seoTitle: How to Embed Typeform in WordPress
metaDescription: Learn how to add a Typeform to WordPress pages or posts using the block editor, custom HTML, or a plugin, with inline and popup options.
intro: Adding an interactive form to a WordPress site can help you collect leads and feedback without sending visitors elsewhere. This guide covers how to get your Typeform embed code and add it to a WordPress page or post, plus the different display styles available.

steps.heading: Create your Typeform
steps.body: Build or open the Typeform you want to add to your site, making sure it's ready to publish before embedding.

steps.heading: Copy the embed code
steps.body: In Typeform's share settings, generate the embed code you'll need to place the form onto your WordPress site.

steps.heading: Add Typeform to WordPress
steps.body: Use the WordPress block editor, a custom HTML block, or a plugin to insert the embed code into the page or post where you want the form to appear.

steps.heading: Customize and test the form
steps.body: Choose between inline, popup, or full-page display styles, then preview the page to confirm the form loads and works as expected.

faq.question: Do I need a plugin to embed Typeform in WordPress?
faq.answer: Not necessarily, since the WordPress block editor supports custom HTML blocks that can hold the embed code, though a plugin can simplify the process for some users.

faq.question: Can I show the form as a popup instead of inline on the page?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform offers different embed styles including inline, popup, and full-page, which you can choose based on how you want it to appear.

faq.question: Will the embedded form match my WordPress site's branding?
faq.answer: You can customize the form's theme and colors in Typeform before embedding it, which helps it fit more naturally with your site's design.

faq.question: Can I embed the same Typeform on multiple pages?
faq.answer: Yes, the same embed code can be reused across different pages or posts on your WordPress site.
